name: upstream-flag-manager-gates-features-via-applyServerFlags
description: Upstream Relay's src/flagManager.ts has applyServerFlags(serverFlags) which filters by flag.override===true and merges over local FeatureFlagDefaults. Upstream-hosted PocketBase pushes override payloads keyed to subscription tier; paying users get true, free/self-host get nothing. Self-hosted PB sends no overrides -> local defaults win -> capabilities with enable*: false in FeatureFlagDefaults appear locked. Upstream leaves ~6 capabilities false-by-default: enableCanvasSync, enableDocumentStatus, enableDocumentHistory, enableNewLinkFormat, enableDiscordLogin, enableVerifyUploads.

name: endpoint-config-modal-requires-tenant-license-jwt
description: EndpointConfigModal -> EndpointManager.addTenant(url, validate=true default) calls fetchTenantLicense which GETs <tenantUrl>/.well-known/relay.md/license expecting a signed JWT (payload: authUrl, apiUrl, customer, logo, environment). Self-host has no such endpoint -> 404 -> 'No tenant license found' error blocks the Add Tenant button. Bypass: addTenant(url, validate=false) + set tenant.apiUrl=tenant.authUrl=tenantUrl, then validateAndSetEndpoints honors unvalidated tenants with explicit endpoints (skip performTenantValidation).

name: pb-hooks-oauth2-redirect-via-code-exchange-poll
description: relay-control-plane/pb_hooks/misc.pb.js overrides PB's built-in GET /api/oauth2-redirect. Flow: receives ?code+?state -> saves record into code_exchange collection keyed by state.slice(0,15) -> returns 200 HTML 'Authentication complete'. Plugin polls code_exchange for the record, then calls pb.authWithOAuth2Code(provider, code, codeVerifier, redirectUrl) to exchange. Custom hook is required because PB 0.22 built-in /api/oauth2-redirect validates against a state cookie that's never set in this cross-window flow — built-in returns 307 to /_/#/auth/oauth2-redirect-failure. Hook MUST be loaded (bind mount healthy) or built-in shadows it.

name: claude-session-transcripts-recover-lost-commits
description: Commits lost to a destructive git op (e.g., submodule deinit + rm -rf .git/modules/X) can be reconstructed from Claude Code session transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/-<slugified-cwd>/*.jsonl. Every Bash 'git commit -m "..."' tool_use preserves the full commit body; every Edit tool_use preserves old_string/new_string pairs. Regex for commit extraction: /git commit[^\n]*?-m\s+(?:"([^"]+)"|'([^']+)'|\\\$\(cat <<'EOF'\\s*\\n(.*?)\\nEOF)/. Hit rate for this session: 9/9 lost fork feature commits recovered (see RECOVERY.md).

LoginManager.refreshToken() calls pb.collection('users').authRefresh().then(...) with no .catch(). When the stored auth token is expired, revoked, or points at a server-deleted record, the 401 surfaces as 'Uncaught (in promise) ClientResponseError 401' at plugin onload. Bug has existed since at least 2026-04-15 in the fork; surfaces only when user happens to have a stale token in LocalAuthStore.
resolution: Add .catch(response => { if (response?.status===401||403) this.logout(); else this.log(...); }). Fixed in commit e00c34e.

description: Upstream Relays.svelte 'Create' button has no in-flight guard. A double/triple/quad-click during the async createRelay POST creates one orphan relay per click. Observed: 4 orphans in <2s from a single impatient user; later observation this session: 27 orphans accumulated across prior sessions.
resolution: creatingRelay flag in PluginSettings.svelte toggled true around the async createRelay call, passed as prop to Relays.svelte which disables the button and swaps label to 'Creating...'. Fixed in commit 4e1f063. Cleanup of existing orphans: DELETE all records in the relays collection via superuser API; storage_quotas orphan separately and need their own sweep.

title: fork-unlocks strategy: flip FeatureFlagDefaults rather than short-circuit applyServerFlags
rationale: Two options considered for unlocking upstream-gated capabilities on self-host: (A) flip 6 FeatureFlagDefaults to true, (B) short-circuit applyServerFlags when pb.baseUrl != upstream. Chose A because: (1) each unlock is visible as a one-line diff in flags.ts — clear git blame, easy to audit against upstream changes; (2) Option B depends on knowing upstream's hosted URL, which can drift; (3) Option A is survives upstream-rebase with mechanical conflict resolution. Cost: any new gated capability upstream adds defaults to false; fork users don't get it until we flip. Acceptable tradeoff given audit clarity.

content: Self-hosted tenant added via EndpointConfigModal with 'Self-hosted' checkbox: authUrl = apiUrl = tenantUrl (one URL for both). Upstream licensed tenants extract apiUrl+authUrl from the signed license JWT payload (usually sibling subdomains). Self-host users should deploy PB at a single URL that serves both the /api/collections auth surface and the /api/* custom routes (pb_hooks). If you want apiUrl separate, patch tenantConfig assignment in EndpointManager.addTenant's !validate branch.

name: recovery-md-documents-reconstructed-fork-commits
description: RECOVERY.md at repo root captures the full recovered rationale for 9 fork feature commits lost when Relay/ submodule was destroyed during the v0.7.5 flatten. Structure: triage ranking, upstream comparison table (what's upstream-native vs fork-only), source-tree status per commit (present/partial/missing), upstream locking mechanism explanation, re-implementation priority list. Companion to HANDOFF.md; corrects HANDOFF's inflated '56 lost commits' figure (real: 9 feature + ~51 Phase-N commits whose code is preserved in 2e32ad7).

name: deferred-fork-unlocks-noStorage-and-leftRelayIds
description: Two fork patches deferred during the v0.7.6 fork-unlock re-apply: (1) noStorage predicate for unmetered self-host (RelayManager should return false when metered===false regardless of quota===0, to hide 'Buy storage' CTA) — tracked as seeds Relay-monorepo-568f. (2) leftRelayIds tracking + post-update() orphan sweep in RelayManager._handleEvent — tracked as seeds Relay-monorepo-b5c9. Both need careful re-reading of upstream's current _handleEvent/update() flow; blind re-implementation risks subtle regressions. Re-attempt when the specific regressions bite in practice.

name: oauth-flow-diagnostic-probe-checklist
description: When OAuth sign-in 400s, diagnostic order: (1) check final browser URL — github.com = GitHub-side rejection (redirect_uri mismatch); localhost:8090/_/#/auth/oauth2-redirect-failure = PB-side redirect-handler rejection; (2) docker exec <ctr> ls /pb/pb_hooks — if empty, bind mount went stale, recreate container; (3) curl -sv http://localhost:8090/api/oauth2-redirect?code=X&state=Y — 200 + Authentication complete HTML = custom hook live; 307 to _/#/auth/oauth2-redirect-failure = PB built-in handling (hook not loaded); (4) docker logs for '[oauth2-redirect] save failed' = code_exchange collection issue.

content: Control-plane operational fixes from this session: (1) when .env changes (e.g. RELAY_SERVER_AUTH rotation), use docker compose up -d --force-recreate — plain docker restart reuses the original env. (2) when pb_hooks stops firing, recreate the control-plane container (bind mount staleness). (3) superuser auth for cleanup: POST /api/admins/auth-with-password with PB_ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD from .env; use returned token in Authorization header (no 'Bearer ' prefix) for subsequent DELETEs. (4) relays collection deletion does NOT cascade to storage_quotas — sweep both collections for a clean slate.
